Updated the `Customer Review Intelligence` flow so it no longer relies on generic “client count/social proof” signals and instead prioritizes source-backed, dated review records from the last 12 months.

### What I reviewed and found

- `browse-ai-reviews` was the core review data source, but it had weak date capture and narrow platform coverage.
- `ChiefMarketingSummary` was using fallback proof markers (`customer_count`, testimonials, trust signals), which is why you were seeing content like “client count signal: 400,000+”.
- `deep-web-recon` and `social-enrichment` only provide keyword/signal hints (not full review intelligence records).
- `semrush-domain-intel` is SEO/PPC/competitor intel only (not customer review intelligence).
- Orchestration in `backend/routes/calibration.py` already passes `browse_ai_reviews` through, so fixing the edge + card logic was the right path.

### Changes made

- In `supabase/functions/browse-ai-reviews/index.ts`:
  - Added better text cleanup and date extraction (`X days/weeks/months ago`, absolute month/day/year, Trustpilot `<time datetime>` support).
  - Added additional customer review sources:
    - forums (Reddit/Quora/forum snippets via search)
    - ProductReview (`productreview.com.au`)
  - Preserved sentiment/rating/date per review record for downstream UI.
- In `frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js`:
  - Replaced proof-signal scoring logic with **dated source review logic** from `browse_ai_reviews.customer_reviews`.
  - Added 12-month filtering and platform/score/sentiment rollups.
  - Rendered **source-backed review entries** (platform, date, sentiment, score if available, source URL).
  - Added **evidence-linked operations action plans** derived from negative themes (speed, support, quality, value).
  - Removed misleading fallback messaging patterns that were causing the incorrect card narrative.

### Why this fixes your issue

The card now enforces “truth from source” behavior for this section:
- It centers on actual review records with source and date.
- It filters to last 12 months when dated evidence exists.
- It does not treat generic client-count/testimonial hints as equivalent to review intelligence depth.

### Validation notes

- Could not run project lint via `npm run lint` (no lint script in this repo).
- Could not run Deno check locally (`deno` not available in this environment).

### Important for your dev site

Because calibration scan data is cached, you’ll need a fresh scan/deploy path for `https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration` to reflect this:
1. Deploy updated edge function `browse-ai-reviews`.
2. Deploy frontend changes.
3. Re-run calibration deep scan with cache bust for the domain/user.

Reference: [BIQc Calibration](https://biqc-web-dev.azurewebsites.net/calibration)
